titleStmt.title: SII 20.1: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 1. (B.K. No. 123 of 1935-36.) BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.hi: On the right and left sides of the Nāga image fixed in the right prākāra-wall of the Madhukēśvara temple.

summary.p: The record is dated in the 12th regnal year, Hēmanta, 7th fortnight, 1st day.

This short inscription in Prakrit language records the gift of a Nāga, a tank and a vihāra by Mahābhuvī the king's daughter Sivakhadanāgasiri (Śivaskandanāgaśrī) whose sons were alive and who was the mother of the prince (yuvarāja). The minister Khadasāti was the superintendent of the work. The Nāga was made by Naṭaka, pupil of [I]damōraka, the Sajayataka (inhabitant of Saṁjayantī).
This is published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXIV, pp. 239-42; cf. also Ind. Ant., Vol. XIV, pp. 331 ff. and Lu7ders' List, No. 1186.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
sidhaṁ raño hāritiputasa viṇhukaḍacuṭukulānaṁdasātākaṁṇisa visasatāya savacharaṁ 10 2 hemaṁtāna pakho 7 divasa 1 mahābhuviya mahārājabālikāya jivaputāyaBu7hler reads this word as jivaputa.
yu varāja[ māAfteryu Bu7hler reads the letters saumā or saomā and restores the word as sakumārāya following Bhagwanlal īndraji.tuya] sivaBu7hler reads saḍa and corrects it to siva.khadanāgasiriya deyadhaṁmaṁ nāgo taḍāgaṁ vihāro ca . Etha kamatiko Amaco sadasāti . sajayatakasa Acariyasa .Bu7hler thinks that one letter letters as jayu. ḥe then restores the word of three letters as pajaya.
damorakasa sisena ṇaṭakena nāgo kato .Bu7hler restores the name as idamorakata.
Digital edition of SII 20.1 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
